sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: IHG Hotels and Resorts kicks off 2026 with new summer sale
feed.summary: IHG Hotels & Resorts has launched its "Your Year in Travel" summer sale, offering 25% off stays for IHG One Rewards members and 20% off for non-members. This limited-time promotion covers over 270 participating hotels across various brands and regions, including destinations in Australia, New Zealand, Japan, and Southeast Asia. The sale runs from January 15 to February 4, 2026, for stays between January 18 and August 31, 2026, with additional perks like 500 bonus points for first-time IHG One Rewards app downloaders.

feed.title: Koninklijke Philips N.V.: How a 133?Year?Old Giant Is Rebooting Healthcare Tech
feed.summary: Koninklijke Philips N.V. is transforming from a consumer electronics brand into a focused health-technology platform, emphasizing diagnostic imaging, patient monitoring, and connected care powered by cloud software and AI. The company aims to address healthcare challenges like clinician shortages and data fragmentation by offering integrated solutions and long-term partnerships rather than standalone devices. This strategic pivot positions Philips to compete with industry giants like GE HealthCare, Siemens Healthineers, and Medtronic, with its stock performance closely tied to successful execution of this health-tech roadmap.

feed.title: West to sell SmartDose 3.5mL system to AbbVie for $112.5 million
feed.summary: West Pharmaceutical Services (NYSE:WST) announced an agreement to sell the manufacturing and supply rights for its SmartDose 3.5mL On-Body Delivery System to AbbVie (NYSE:ABBV) for $112.5 million. This transaction, expected to close in mid-2026, includes associated facilities and represents about 4% of West's anticipated 2025 revenue. AbbVie's acquisition aligns with its strategic expansion in drug delivery, while West will continue commercial supply until closing and retain rights for other SmartDose versions.

feed.title: Principal Financial's Q4 2025 Earnings: What to Expect
feed.summary: Principal Financial Group (PFG) is set to release its Q4 2025 earnings, with analysts expecting a 15% increase in adjusted EPS to $2.23. Despite a recent earnings miss, the company's shares have gained 15.3% over the past 52 weeks, though trailing the S&P 500. Analysts currently hold a "Hold" rating on PFG stock.

feed.title: A Look At Broadridge Financial Solutions (BR) Valuation After DeepSee Agentic AI Partnership Expansion
feed.summary: Broadridge Financial Solutions (BR) has expanded its partnership with DeepSee, investing in the company to integrate agentic AI into post-trade operations. While the stock is up only 1.3% year-to-date, Simply Wall St's analysis suggests it is undervalued by 17.1% based on core business strength, yet its P/E ratio is higher than industry averages, indicating the market is already paying a premium for its quality and AI initiative.

feed.title: USA Compression Partners stock price target raised to $25 from $23 at Mizuho
feed.summary: Mizuho increased its price target for USA Compression Partners (NYSE: USAC) from $23 to $25, while maintaining a Neutral rating. The firm noted USA Compression's outperformance against peers, attributed to positive natural gas macro trends and increased exposure to dry gas basins. Recent performance includes a 14.5% one-year total return and a "nicely accretive" acquisition of J-W Power, which is expected to accelerate deleveraging and improve margins.

feed.title: JPMorgan Adjusts PT on Expeditors International of Washington to $132 From $134, Maintains Underweight Rating
feed.summary: JPMorgan has revised its price target for Expeditors International of Washington (EXPD) to $132 from $134, while reiterating an Underweight rating on the stock. This adjustment reflects a bearish outlook from the firm despite other analysts like BofA and Wolfe making various adjustments to their ratings and price targets for the logistics company. The article also provides a company profile, financial data, and analyst consensus information for Expeditors International.

feed.title: MGG.DE MGM Resorts (XETRA) +13.99% intraday 12 Jan 2026: earnings catalysts
feed.summary: MGM Resorts International (MGG.DE) saw an intraday surge of 13.99% on XETRA, driven by better-than-expected quarterly results, renewed optimism for travel demand, and sector rotation into entertainment stocks. While short-term momentum looks strong with the stock trading above key moving averages, Meyka AI's forecast model projects a significant downside in the long term. Investors are advised to consider both the immediate trading opportunities and longer-term risks like debt levels and margin pressure.

feed.title: Targa Resources stock price target raised to $196 by Goldman Sachs
feed.summary: Goldman Sachs has increased its price target for Targa Resources (NYSE:TRGP) to $196 while maintaining a Buy rating, citing strong Permian volume growth and successful acquisitions. The investment bank's EBITDA forecasts for Targa in Q4 2025, full-year 2025, and 2026 are largely in line with or above company guidance and consensus, driven by strategic acquisitions and anticipated growth in the Permian basin. Other analysts from UBS, Wells Fargo, and RBC Capital have also raised their price targets for Targa Resources, reflecting positive sentiment around the company's recent strategic moves and operational performance.

feed.title: Bank of America discloses 1.43% stake in Avadel Pharmaceuticals
feed.summary: Bank of America has disclosed a 1.43% stake in Avadel Pharmaceuticals plc, as revealed in a recent regulatory filing under Irish Takeover Panel Act rules. The holding includes both owned shares and cash-settled derivatives, with the bank also maintaining short positions in the company. These transactions, including purchases and sales of shares, were conducted on January 9, 2026.

feed.title: Barclays Adjusts PT on Teledyne Technologies to $579 From $584, Maintains Equalweight Rating
feed.summary: Barclays has adjusted its price target for Teledyne Technologies (TDY) to $579, down from $584, while maintaining an Equalweight rating on the stock. This update reflects a minor revision in the analyst's valuation of the company. The article also provides a summary of recent news and financial data related to Teledyne Technologies.

feed.title: Barclays Adjusts Price Target on Paycom Software to $185 From $210, Maintains Equalweight Rating
feed.summary: Barclays has lowered its price target on Paycom Software Inc. (PAYC) to $185 from $210, while maintaining an "Equalweight" rating on the stock. This adjustment comes after other financial institutions like Citigroup, UBS, KeyBanc, Baird, BMO Capital, Guggenheim, TD Cowen, Deutsche Bank, Stifel, Jefferies, JPMorgan, and Mizuho also revised their price targets for Paycom in recent months. The company, which specializes in cloud-based human resources management software, recently reported its Q3 2025 earnings with rising non-GAAP earnings and revenue but slower quarterly revenue growth.

feed.title: Earnings Scheduled For January 12, 2026
feed.summary: This article lists companies scheduled to report earnings on January 12, 2026. Sify Technologies (NASDAQ: SIFY) is expected to report a quarterly loss of $0.01 per share on revenue of $171.10 million. Wealthfront (NASDAQ: WLTH) is estimated to report quarterly earnings of $0.20 per share on revenue of $93.18 million.

feed.title: Dollar Tree Signal 12/01: Plunging Margins (Chart)
feed.summary: This article analyzes Dollar Tree (NASDAQ: DLTR) stock, suggesting a short trade opportunity due to plunging margins and high valuations despite a strong 2025 rally. The analysis highlights bearish technical indicators, elevated valuations, and concerns regarding sustained growth and profitability outlooks. The author provides specific entry, take profit, and stop loss levels for a short position.

feed.title: AbbVie to buy Arizona manufacturing facility from West Pharmaceutical (WST:NYSE)
feed.summary: AbbVie (ABBV) has announced an agreement to acquire a device manufacturing facility in Tempe, Arizona, along with related intellectual property, from West Pharmaceutical Services (WST). West Pharmaceutical will divest all manufacturing and supply rights for its SmartDose 3.5mL On-Body Delivery System as part of this transaction. This acquisition marks a strategic move for AbbVie to enhance its manufacturing capabilities.

feed.title: AbbVie to acquire West Pharmaceutical’s Arizona facility for $175 million
feed.summary: AbbVie announced its intent to acquire a device manufacturing facility in Tempe, Arizona, from West Pharmaceutical Services for $175 million. This acquisition, which includes associated intellectual property and 3.5 mL on-body injector technology, aims to strengthen AbbVie's manufacturing capabilities for immunology and neuroscience medicines and expand its U.S. presence. The transaction is expected to close in mid-2026 and aligns with AbbVie’s commitment to invest over $10 billion in the U.S.

feed.title: KLA Corp. price target raised to $1,500 from $1,125 at Susquehanna
feed.summary: Susquehanna has increased its price target for KLA Corp. (KLAC) to $1,500 from $1,125, maintaining a Neutral rating. This adjustment comes as channel checks indicate the semiconductor capital equipment industry is poised for significant spending in wafer fab equipment, expecting global semi revenues to exceed $1 trillion. The firm believes that fundamental trends in wafer fab equipment remain strong, necessitating substantial investment in the supply chain.

feed.title: ‘Could have been a hell of a lot worse’: At Nebraska plant, ADM allowed dangerous dust to build up beyond safe levels, recordings show
feed.summary: A 2022 dust explosion at an ADM plant in Fremont, Nebraska, revealed that the facility had accumulated combustible grain dust eight times the acceptable federal limit. Newly obtained OSHA interview recordings show that senior ADM managers were unaware of federal safety standards, few employees were disciplined for safety lapses, and dangerous cleaning practices like using compressed air on running machinery were common. ADM paid a fine of about $41,000 for the incident, and this event is part of a pattern of dust explosions at ADM facilities across the Midwest, with at least eight documented between 2018 and 2024.

feed.title: Seagate 32TB Capacities Now Shipping to Channel and Retail Partners Globally
feed.summary: Seagate Technology is shipping its new 32TB SkyHawk AI, Exos, and IronWolf Pro hard drives to channel and retail partners globally. These high-capacity drives are designed to meet the escalating data storage demands driven by AI applications, particularly in video analytics, at both the edge and in data centers. The company is showcasing these solutions at Intersec 2026, emphasizing their role in transforming video data into actionable intelligence.

feed.title: Drugmaker bringing 200 jobs to Arizona plant for next-gen medicines
feed.summary: AbbVie (NYSE:ABBV) announced its agreement to acquire a device manufacturing facility and associated intellectual property from West Pharmaceutical Services (NYSE:WST) in Tempe, Arizona. This strategic move aims to expand AbbVie's drug delivery device manufacturing capabilities and capacity, particularly for current and next-generation immunology and neuroscience medicines. The company plans to invest over $175 million in the facility and create approximately 200 new jobs, with the transaction expected to close in mid-2026.

feed.title: West Signs Agreement to Sell the Manufacturing and Supply Rights for SmartDose® 3.5mL On-Body Delivery System to AbbVie
feed.summary: West Pharmaceutical Services, Inc. announced a definitive agreement to sell all manufacturing and supply rights for its SmartDose® 3.5mL On-Body Delivery System to AbbVie for $112.5 million. This strategic decision aligns with West's commitment to its customer development pipeline and patient-centric approach for large-dose on-body delivery devices, allowing them to focus on other SmartDose versions. The transaction is expected to close in mid-2026, with West maintaining commercial supply until then.

feed.title: Exclusive | UnitedHealth Used Aggressive Tactics to Boost Medicare Payments, Senate Report Finds
feed.summary: A Senate committee investigation found that UnitedHealth Group used aggressive tactics to collect payment-boosting diagnoses for its Medicare Advantage members. The federal government pays insurers a lump sum for Medicare Advantage, with additional payments for patients with costly medical conditions, a process known as risk adjustment. This investigation was launched following Wall Street Journal reports examining the company's Medicare Advantage practices.

feed.title: New partnership aims to widen investment choices for savers in the Gulf
feed.summary: First Abu Dhabi Bank (FAB) and T. Rowe Price have formed a strategic partnership to expand investment solutions across the GCC, targeting retail, private banking, and institutional clients. T. Rowe Price will act as FAB's investment partner, offering expertise across various asset classes including equity, fixed income, alternatives, and multi-asset strategies. This collaboration aims to broaden FAB’s wealth and investment franchise, deliver tailored solutions, and support the development of the GCC's asset management market.

feed.title: First Abu Dhabi Bank and T. Rowe Price Enter into a Strategic Partnership to Enhance Investment Offering across the GCC
feed.summary: First Abu Dhabi Bank (FAB) has announced a strategic partnership with T. Rowe Price, a global asset management firm, to enhance its investment offerings across the GCC region. T. Rowe Price will act as FAB's investment partner, providing diverse investment solutions to retail, private banking, and institutional clients. This collaboration aims to deliver tailored, high-value investment solutions and strengthen FAB's position as a leading financial partner in the region.

feed.title: AI, Medicaid cuts and payer no-shows: 3 things to watch at JPM 2026
feed.summary: The 2026 J.P. Morgan Healthcare Conference is set to begin, with artificial intelligence, Medicaid cuts, and the notable absence of major health insurers dominating discussions. AI's role has evolved, with companies like Anthropic and Nvidia making significant healthcare announcements, focusing on real ROI. Nonprofit health systems will address impending Medicaid cuts, while the limited presence of major payers, possibly due to security concerns following a recent industry tragedy, highlights shifts in the conference's dynamics.

feed.title: Nisa Investment Advisors LLC Reduces Holdings in IDEX Corporation $IEX
feed.summary: Nisa Investment Advisors LLC significantly reduced its stake in IDEX Corporation, selling 17.9% of its holdings in Q3, bringing its total to 28,562 shares valued at approximately $4.65 million. Despite this reduction by one institutional investor, other firms acquired additional shares in IDEX. The company has authorized a $1.0 billion share repurchase program, declared a quarterly dividend of $0.71, and maintains a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating from analysts with an average price target of $196.86.

feed.title: Nisa Investment Advisors LLC Has $5.58 Million Stock Position in Huntington Bancshares Incorporated $HBAN
feed.summary: Nisa Investment Advisors LLC reduced its stake in Huntington Bancshares (HBAN) by 18.7% in Q3, selling 73,579 shares but still holding 320,366 shares valued at $5.584 million. Despite institutional investors owning over 80% of the stock, insiders sold 97,863 shares recently. Huntington Bancshares maintains a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating with an average target price of $20.03, and offers a quarterly dividend of $0.155, yielding 3.4%.

feed.title: Delta Air Lines, Inc.$DAL Shares Sold by Nisa Investment Advisors LLC
feed.summary: Nisa Investment Advisors LLC reduced its stake in Delta Air Lines by 21.6% in the third quarter, selling 28,524 shares, though other institutional investors increased their holdings. Analysts have raised price targets for Delta, boosting investor sentiment. Insider selling by EVP John E. Laughter and SVP William C. Carroll also occurred, reducing their company stock ownership.

feed.title: Pentagon funds NioCorp project as cash pile hits $307M
feed.summary: NioCorp (NASDAQ:NB) reported preliminary unaudited financial results for the three and six-month periods ending December 31, 2025, highlighting a record cash balance of $307 million and an award of up to $10 million from the U.S. Department of Defense for its Elk Creek Critical Minerals Project. Despite positive project advancements and significant funding, the company posted a six-month net loss of $43.4 million, driven partly by non-cash earnout and warrant revaluations. NioCorp is actively progressing on its Elk Creek project with an infill drilling campaign, acquisition of scandium alloy manufacturing assets, and land purchases, while also entering agreements with the DoD and Lockheed Martin for scandium technologies.

feed.title: Nisa Investment Advisors LLC Cuts Stake in Brown & Brown, Inc. $BRO
feed.summary: Nisa Investment Advisors LLC significantly reduced its stake in Brown & Brown, Inc. (NYSE:BRO) by 29% in the third quarter, selling over 20,000 shares. Despite this, Brown & Brown reported strong Q3 earnings, surpassing analyst expectations, and increased its quarterly dividend. Institutional investors collectively own a substantial 71.01% of the company's stock.

feed.title: Nisa Investment Advisors LLC Acquires 114,946 Shares of Annaly Capital Management Inc $NLY
feed.summary: Nisa Investment Advisors LLC substantially increased its stake in Annaly Capital Management Inc. (NYSE:NLY) by 73.9% in the third quarter, purchasing an additional 114,946 shares, bringing its total ownership to 270,482 shares valued at approximately $5.68 million. Annaly Capital Management has a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating from analysts with an average target price of $22.63, although its high dividend payout ratio of 129.63% raises concerns about sustainability despite a 12.0% yield.

feed.title: Nisa Investment Advisors LLC Reduces Position in SBA Communications Corporation $SBAC
feed.summary: Nisa Investment Advisors LLC significantly reduced its stake in SBA Communications Corporation (NASDAQ:SBAC) by 28.2% in Q3, selling 9,182 shares. Despite this, institutional investors still own 97.35% of the company's stock. Analysts have given SBAC a "Moderate Buy" rating with an average price target of $237.00, while the company reported better-than-expected earnings and pays a quarterly dividend of $1.11 per share.

feed.title: Nisa Investment Advisors LLC Trims Stock Position in NVR, Inc. $NVR
feed.summary: Nisa Investment Advisors LLC reduced its stake in NVR, Inc. by 21.9% in the third quarter of 2025, selling 175 shares and holding 625 shares valued at $5.02 million. This reduction comes as NVR exceeded Q3 earnings and revenue estimates, reporting $112.33 EPS and $2.64 billion in revenue. Despite a mixed market outlook and a "Moderate Buy" consensus from analysts, institutional investors own a significant portion of the stock, and an insider recently purchased shares.

feed.title: OneSpaWorld Holdings (NASDAQ:OSW) Might Have The Makings Of A Multi-Bagger
feed.summary: OneSpaWorld Holdings (NASDAQ:OSW) has demonstrated signs of being a potential multi-bagger stock due to its increasing Return on Capital Employed (ROCE). The company has become profitable, achieving a 13% ROCE, despite capital employed remaining steady. Investors have already seen a significant 124% return over the last five years, indicating growing recognition of these positive changes.

feed.title: State of Alaska Department of Revenue Sells 27,176 Shares of Exxon Mobil Corporation $XOM
feed.summary: The State of Alaska Department of Revenue reduced its stake in Exxon Mobil (NYSE:XOM) by 4.2% in the third quarter of 2025, selling 27,176 shares and holding 616,135 shares valued at $69.47 million. Despite Exxon Mobil increasing its quarterly dividend to $1.03, the company warned of a potential $800M–$1.2B cut in Q4 upstream profit due to lower liquids/crude prices. Analysts have a consensus "Hold" rating with an average target price of $129.71 for XOM, which currently trades near $124.60 with a market cap of $525.4 billion.

feed.title: Oregon Public Employees Retirement Fund Has $3.58 Million Stake in United Airlines Holdings Inc $UAL
feed.summary: The Oregon Public Employees Retirement Fund significantly increased its stake in United Airlines Holdings Inc. (NASDAQ:UAL) by 31.3% in the third quarter, now owning 37,053 shares valued at approximately $3.58 million. This comes as United Airlines reported strong Q3 earnings, beating EPS expectations, and analysts maintained a "Moderate Buy" rating with an average target price of $134.33, despite some insider selling.

feed.title: LG Display chief vows to counter China's rise by reducing costs for OLED products
feed.summary: LG Display CEO Jeong Chul-dong announced plans to combat China's increased competition in the LCD market by enhancing the price competitiveness of its premium OLED panels. He emphasized the technological superiority of OLEDs and the company's commitment to cost reduction and AI integration to ensure stable profitability. LG Display also aims to leverage its P-OLED technology for the growing humanoid robot industry.

feed.title: State of Alaska Department of Revenue Increases Stock Position in Teledyne Technologies Incorporated $TDY
feed.summary: The State of Alaska Department of Revenue has significantly increased its stake in Teledyne Technologies Incorporated (NYSE:TDY) by 52.1% in the third quarter of 2025, now holding 8,016 shares valued at approximately $4.697 million. This move is part of broader institutional investor activity, with major funds like Vanguard, State Street, Dodge & Cox, and Norges Bank also holding substantial positions, collectively owning 91.58% of the company's stock. Teledyne Technologies, which recently beat earnings estimates and provided optimistic FY 2025 guidance, carries a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating from analysts with an average target price of $601.38.

feed.title: Oregon Public Employees Retirement Fund Grows Position in Microchip Technology Incorporated $MCHP
feed.summary: The Oregon Public Employees Retirement Fund significantly increased its stake in Microchip Technology Incorporated (MCHP) by 32.8% in Q3, now holding 61,686 shares valued at approximately $3.96 million. This increase aligns with Microchip's positive Q4 sales guidance and new product developments, such as custom firmware for NVIDIA, which led to a stock rally and several analyst upgrades. The company has a consensus "Moderate Buy" rating and a price target of $77.62, reflecting strong institutional interest and positive market sentiment.

feed.title: Pan American Silver PAAS stock jumps in premarket as silver smashes records — what to watch next
feed.summary: Pan American Silver (PAAS) stock rose by 4.5% in premarket trading after silver futures surged 6%, driven by increased investor interest in hard assets due to geopolitical tensions and a criminal probe involving the Federal Reserve Chair. The company's next significant events include upcoming conferences later this month and its Q4 and year-end 2025 financial results on February 18th. Investors are monitoring whether silver prices will maintain their gains, as well as company-specific risks like mining costs and operational challenges.

feed.title: Cardinal Health stock price target raised to $222 from $210 at Mizuho
feed.summary: Mizuho has increased its price target for Cardinal Health (NYSE:CAH) to $222 from $210, reaffirming an Outperform rating. This adjustment reflects expectations for continued strong operating trends in 2025 and 2026 for major drug distributors, driven by factors like favorable brand drug price inflation, a robust pipeline of generic drug launches, and sustained merger and acquisition activity in the Specialty MSO industry. The company also reported strong fiscal year 2026 earnings, including higher-than-expected EPS and revenue, and completed the acquisition of Solaris Health.

feed.title: SG Americas Securities LLC Purchases 25,216 Shares of Dover Corporation $DOV
feed.summary: SG Americas Securities LLC significantly increased its stake in Dover Corporation (NYSE:DOV) by 1,644.9% in the third quarter, acquiring an additional 25,216 shares. Other institutional investors have also adjusted their positions in Dover, while some corporate insiders have sold shares. The company has a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating from analysts with an average target price of $217.09, and recently announced a quarterly dividend.

feed.title: Hong Kong’s housing market gets a vote of confidence from major banks
feed.summary: Major financial institutions like Citi and Bank of America are expressing increased optimism about Hong Kong's residential property market for the current year. Citi has raised its home price forecast to 8 percent, up from 3 percent, citing lower mortgage rates, strong leasing demand, supply constraints, and a faster-than-expected rebound. Analysts expect a multi-year upcycle, with home sales potentially exceeding new completions, driven by structural demand from rising student numbers and skilled worker inflows.

feed.title: What to Expect From Lam Research’s Q2 2025 Earnings Report
feed.summary: Lam Research Corporation (LRCX) is set to announce its Q2 2026 earnings on January 28, with analysts projecting a 27.5% increase in EPS to $1.16. The semiconductor giant has consistently beaten Wall Street estimates and its shares have significantly outperformed the S&P 500, buoyed by positive analyst ratings and optimistic industry forecasts for the semiconductor sector. Despite trading above its mean price target, analysts still see further upside potential for LRCX.

feed.title: General Motors takes US$6 billion EV-related writedown
feed.summary: General Motors recorded US$6.0 billion in charges related to its electric vehicle (EV) business in the last quarter of 2025. These charges include non-cash impairments and other non-cash charges totaling US$1.8 billion, and an additional US$4.2 billion in charges with a cash impact. The writedown follows a review of the company's EV capacity and investments, driven by softened US demand and changes in federal policy like the removal of consumer EV tax credits.

feed.title: Raymond James reiterates Strong Buy on Aardvark Therapeutics stock with $47 target
feed.summary: Raymond James has reiterated a Strong Buy rating and a $47 price target for Aardvark Therapeutics Inc. (NASDAQ: AARD), citing confidence in its lead asset ARD-101 for Prader-Willi Syndrome, despite existing treatments. The firm highlighted promising Phase 2 efficacy and risk-reducing elements in the ongoing Phase 3 HERO trial, with data expected in Q3 2026. The company also plans to start trials for obesity programs in H1 2026, which are not yet included in the valuation.

feed.title: JPMorgan Adjusts Price Target on American Express to $385 From $360, Maintains Neutral Rating
feed.summary: JPMorgan has increased its price target for American Express (AXP) to $385 from $360, while retaining a Neutral rating on the stock. This adjustment reflects an updated outlook from the financial institution on the consumer lending company. The article also briefly mentions recent activities of other analysts and market news related to American Express.

feed.title: Morgan Stanley Upgrades Akamai Technologies to Overweight From Underweight, Adjusts PT to $115 From $83
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley has upgraded Akamai Technologies (AKAM) from Underweight to Overweight and increased its price target from $83 to $115. This positive adjustment reflects an improved outlook on the content delivery network and cybersecurity company. The article also lists other recent analyst activities and company news related to Akamai.

feed.title: Mondi cut to “underweight” as Morgan Stanley flags valuation risk; shares fall
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley downgraded Mondi to "underweight" from "equal weight" due to valuation concerns and reduced earnings visibility, causing Mondi's shares to drop over 3%. The brokerage cited group margins falling below global financial crisis levels and suboptimal operating rates in end markets, leading to a cut in EBITDA forecasts for 2026. Morgan Stanley projects Mondi's 2026 EBITDA at €958 million, significantly below the consensus of €1.13 billion, and noted the stock is trading at a premium to its historical valuation.

feed.title: Spire Wealth Management Sells 7,665 Shares of Marathon Petroleum Corporation $MPC
feed.summary: Spire Wealth Management reduced its stake in Marathon Petroleum Corporation (NYSE:MPC) by 77.0% in the third quarter, selling 7,665 shares and ending up with 2,288 shares valued at $441,000. Several other institutional investors also adjusted their positions in MPC, with some increasing their holdings. Marathon Petroleum recently increased its quarterly dividend to $1.00 per share and reported strong earnings, exceeding analyst expectations.
